name: DESeq2 Pseudobulk Differential Expression

cell_type_key.guidance: obs column with cell type labels. DE is run separately per cell type.

sample_key.guidance: obs column identifying biological samples/donors. These are the REPLICATES.

condition_key.guidance: obs column with condition labels (e.g., 'disease' vs 'healthy').

min_cells_per_pseudobulk.guidance: Minimum cells needed to form a valid pseudobulk sample. Cell type × sample combinations with fewer cells are excluded.

design_formula.guidance: DESeq2 design formula. Can add covariates: '~ condition + sex + age'. The last term is the variable of interest.

outputs.de_results: DataFrame per cell type with gene, log2FoldChange, pvalue, padj
outputs.n_de_genes: Number of significant DE genes per cell type

validation.check: At least 2 biological replicates per condition per cell type. Report pseudobulk sample sizes.

notes: CRITICAL: NEVER use individual cells as replicates. Always aggregate to pseudobulk (sum raw counts per cell type per sample). Biological replicates are SAMPLES, not cells. This is the #1 error in cross-condition single-cell DE analysis. Pseudobulk methods (DESeq2, edgeR) consistently outperform cell-level methods (Wilcoxon, MAST) for cross-condition comparisons (Squair et al. 2021, Nature Communications).
